ORDER RE: DEFENDANTS TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA AND THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF ABDOL NOVIN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA, THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF ABDOL NOVIN. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. LEAVE IS GRANTED PER MR. NOVIN'S REQUEST TO AMEND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD ADDITIONAL ALLEGATIONS REGARDING THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE TRAVELER COMPANIES, INC. AND TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA. MR. NOVIN HAS ADEQUATELY PLED FACTS TO ALLEGE A BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING CLAIM AGAINST TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA. ORDER SIGNED. (302/HEK) JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA & THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT'S DELAY AND/OR DENIAL IN PAYING BENEFITS WAS REASONABLE. FOR EXAMPLE, MR. PARRISH ORIGINALLY ESTIMATED THE REPAIRS AT APPROXIMATELY $478,000 AND DEFENDANT HAS SUBSEQUENTLY PAID ROUGHLY $1.6 MILLION DOLLARS IN BENEFITS TO DATE. THE TRIER OF FACT COULD FIND THAT THIS GREAT DISPARITY INDICATES BAD FAITH. IN ADDITION, THE TRIER OF FACT COULD CONCLUDE THAT DEFENDANT'S PAYMENTS WERE UNREASONABLE IN LIGHT OF THE APPRAISAL AWARD. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT IS "INDISPUTABLE THAT THE BASIS FOR THE INSURER'S DENIAL OF BENEFITS WAS REASONABLE." WILSON V. 21ST CENTURY INS. CO. (2007) 42 CAL.4TH 713, 724. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT AN OFFICER, DIRECTOR, OR MANAGING AGENT NEEDED TO ACT MALICIOUSLY BECAUSE THE SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT RAISE THAT ISSUE AND THAT ARGUMENT WAS RAISED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THE REPLY BRIEF. THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. 2 ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-26-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. J. EDWARD KERLEY OF HEREFORD KERLEY LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 379-5801 EXT. 210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARJIE D. BARROWS OF FORAN GLENNON & RUDLOFF P.C., TEL. NO. (510) 740-1500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA & THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant failed to maintain its initial summary adjudication burden of production. Defendant's evidence shows that there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's delay and/or denial in paying benefits was reasonable. For example, Mr. Parrish originally estimated the repairs at approximately $478,000 and defendant has subsequently paid roughly $1.6 million dollars in benefits to date. The trier of fact could find that this great disparity indicates bad faith. In addition, the trier of fact could conclude that defendant's payments were unreasonable in light of the appraisal award. Defendant has not shown that it is "indisputable that the basis for the insurer's denial of benefits was reasonable." Wilson v. 21st Century Ins. Co. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 713, 724. The court denies defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages because defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The Court rejects defendant's argument that an officer, director, or managing agent needed to act maliciously because the separate statement does not raise that issue and that argument was raised for the first time in the reply brief. The evidentiary objections were overruled. 2 ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EHG

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA, THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF ABDOL NOVIN.
Appearances: Marjie D. Barrows of Foran Glennon Palandech Ponzi & Rudloff PC (510.740.1500) for defendants and moving parties; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Overruled as to the first cause of action and sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Leave is granted per Mr. Novin's request to amend the second cause of action to plead additional allegations regarding the relationship between The Traveler Companies, Inc. and Travelers Property Casualty Company of America. Mr. Novin has adequately pled facts to allege a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing claim against Travelers Property Casualty Company of America. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge Harold Kahn; Not reported
